---
title: "Join External Validation Sheets"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
suppressWarnings(suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(readxl)
  library(dplyr)
  library(readr)
  library(stringr)
}))
```

```{r paths}
resolve_project_path <- function(...) {
  candidates <- c(
    normalizePath(file.path(...), winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE),
    normalizePath(file.path("..", ...), winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
  )

  existing_candidate <- candidates[file.exists(candidates)][1]
  if (!is.na(existing_candidate)) {
    return(existing_candidate)
  }

  candidates[1]
}

external_validation_dir <- resolve_project_path("data", "externalvalidation")
input_file <- file.path(external_validation_dir, "alumnivalidation.xlsx")
output_file <- file.path(external_validation_dir, "ksb_alumni_linkedin_joined.csv")

if (!file.exists(input_file)) {
  stop("Could not find input workbook: ", input_file)
}
```

```{r read-sheets}
available_sheets <- excel_sheets(input_file)

alumni_sheet <- if ("ksb_alumni" %in% available_sheets) {
  "ksb_alumni"
} else if ("ksd_alumni" %in% available_sheets) {
  "ksd_alumni"
} else {
  stop("Could not find alumni sheet. Expected ksb_alumni or ksd_alumni.")
}

linkedin_sheet <- "ksb_linkedin"
if (!linkedin_sheet %in% available_sheets) {
  stop("Could not find LinkedIn sheet: ", linkedin_sheet)
}

ksb_alumni <- suppressWarnings(read_excel(input_file, sheet = alumni_sheet))

linkedin_col_types <- rep("guess", 29)
linkedin_col_types[c(13, 24, 25, 29)] <- "date"
linkedin_col_types[28] <- "text"

ksb_linkedin <- suppressWarnings(read_excel(
  input_file,
  sheet = linkedin_sheet,
  col_types = linkedin_col_types
))
```

```{r join-and-save}
required_join_column <- "Index"

if (!required_join_column %in% names(ksb_alumni) ||
    !required_join_column %in% names(ksb_linkedin)) {
  stop("Both sheets must contain an Index column for joining.")
}

wide_experience_columns <- unlist(lapply(
  c("company_name", "company_industry", "job_description", "title"),
  function(prefix) paste0(prefix, "_", 1:27)
))

expected_alumni_columns <- c(
  "Index",
  "City",
  "State",
  "Country",
  "Job_Title",
  "Employer",
  "Grad_Year",
  "Degree",
  "Degree_Program",
  "Degree_Major"
)

expected_linkedin_columns <- c(
  "Index",
  "field_of_study",
  "education_start_year",
  "education_end_year",
  "current_job_title",
  "current_job_description",
  "current_company_name",
  "current_company_url",
  "Company_size",
  "current_job_industry",
  "current_job_country",
  "current_job_location",
  "current_job_start_date",
  "employment_type",
  "is_currently_employed",
  "current_job_duration_years",
  "previous_job_title",
  "previous_job_description",
  "previous_company_size",
  "previous_company_name",
  "previous_job_country",
  "previous_job_location",
  "previous_job_Industry",
  "previous_job_start_date",
  "previous_job_end_date",
  "total_experience_years",
  "Skills",
  "total_experience_count",
  "last_scraped_date",
  wide_experience_columns
)

add_missing_columns <- function(df, required_columns) {
  missing_columns <- setdiff(required_columns, names(df))

  for (column_name in missing_columns) {
    df[[column_name]] <- NA
  }

  df
}

missing_alumni_columns <- setdiff(expected_alumni_columns, names(ksb_alumni))
missing_linkedin_columns <- setdiff(expected_linkedin_columns, names(ksb_linkedin))

ksb_alumni <- add_missing_columns(ksb_alumni, expected_alumni_columns)
ksb_linkedin <- add_missing_columns(ksb_linkedin, expected_linkedin_columns)

exact_join_key_report <- tibble::tibble(
  alumni_column = "Index",
  linkedin_column = "Index",
  action = "Used as the only merge key"
)

conceptually_similar_columns_report <- tibble::tribble(
  ~alumni_column,           ~linkedin_column,                           ~action,
  "Job_Title",              "current_job_title",                        "Keep both; LinkedIn is richer for modeling",
  "Employer",               "current_company_name",                     "Keep both",
  "City / State / Country", "current_job_location / current_job_country", "Keep both; standardize later if needed"
)

alumni_columns_report <- tibble::tibble(
  source_sheet = "ksb_alumni",
  column = expected_alumni_columns,
  role = "Academic, demographic, or structured context"
)

linkedin_modeling_columns_report <- tibble::tibble(
  source_sheet = "ksb_linkedin",
  column = expected_linkedin_columns,
  role = if_else(
    column %in% c(
      "current_job_title",
      "current_job_description",
      "previous_job_title",
      "previous_job_description",
      "Skills",
      wide_experience_columns
    ),
    "Text-heavy modeling input",
    "LinkedIn profile context"
  )
)

missing_column_report <- tibble::tibble(
  sheet = c("alumni", "linkedin"),
  missing_columns_created = c(
    paste(missing_alumni_columns, collapse = ", "),
    paste(missing_linkedin_columns, collapse = ", ")
  )
) %>%
  mutate(
    missing_columns_created = if_else(
      missing_columns_created == "",
      "None",
      missing_columns_created
    )
  )

unmatched_linkedin_rows <- ksb_linkedin %>%
  anti_join(ksb_alumni, by = "Index")

if (nrow(unmatched_linkedin_rows) > 0) {
  stop(nrow(unmatched_linkedin_rows), " LinkedIn rows did not match an alumni Index.")
}

joined_external_validation <- ksb_alumni %>%
  left_join(ksb_linkedin, by = "Index")

if (nrow(joined_external_validation) != nrow(ksb_alumni)) {
  stop("Joined row count does not equal alumni row count.")
}

joined_row_summary <- tibble::tibble(
  row_type = c("Alumni rows with LinkedIn match", "Alumni rows without LinkedIn match"),
  row_count = c(
    sum(joined_external_validation$Index %in% ksb_linkedin$Index),
    sum(!joined_external_validation$Index %in% ksb_linkedin$Index)
  )
)

final_structure_report <- tibble::tribble(
  ~section, ~columns,
  "Join key", "Index",
  "Alumni columns", paste(setdiff(expected_alumni_columns, "Index"), collapse = ", "),
  "LinkedIn columns", paste(setdiff(expected_linkedin_columns, "Index"), collapse = ", ")
)

write_csv(joined_external_validation, output_file, na = "")

message("Wrote ", nrow(joined_external_validation), " rows and ",
        ncol(joined_external_validation), " columns to ", output_file)
message("Matched ", joined_row_summary$row_count[1],
        " LinkedIn rows to alumni records.")
```

```{r exact-join-key}
exact_join_key_report
```

```{r conceptually-similar-columns}
conceptually_similar_columns_report
```

```{r alumni-columns-introduced}
alumni_columns_report
```

```{r linkedin-modeling-columns}
linkedin_modeling_columns_report
```

```{r joined-row-summary}
joined_row_summary
```

```{r final-structure}
final_structure_report
```

```{r joined-rows-preview}
joined_external_validation %>%
  filter(Index %in% ksb_linkedin$Index) %>%
  select(
    Index,
    City,
    State,
    Country,
    current_job_country,
    current_job_location,
    Job_Title,
    current_job_title,
    Employer,
    current_company_name,
    Grad_Year,
    education_end_year,
    Degree_Major,
    field_of_study,
    current_job_description,
    previous_job_description,
    Skills
  ) %>%
  head(25)
```

```{r missing-source-column-details}
missing_source_columns_created <- bind_rows(
  tibble::tibble(
    sheet = "alumni",
    column_created = missing_alumni_columns
  ),
  tibble::tibble(
    sheet = "linkedin",
    column_created = missing_linkedin_columns
  )
)

if (nrow(missing_source_columns_created) == 0) {
  missing_source_columns_created <- tibble::tibble(
    sheet = c("alumni", "linkedin"),
    column_created = "No missing columns were created"
  )
}

missing_source_columns_created
```

```{r preview}
head(joined_external_validation)
```
